basics.description:
Deploy **UTL-X Transformation Engine** as a managed application in your Azure subscription. Transform JSON, XML, CSV, and YAML messages at 86K+ msg/s with 652 built-in functions.

Billing is handled automatically through your Azure bill. A 14-day free trial is included.

location.label: Region
location.toolTip: Azure region for all resources. Choose a region close to your workloads.

options.text: UTL-X transforms data between JSON, XML, CSV, YAML, and OData natively — without converting to XML first. Supports 652 stdlib functions, schema validation, and pipeline chaining.

elements.name: tier
elements.label: Tier
elements.toolTip: Determines the number of concurrent worker threads per instance. In a typical integration flow, each transformation step uses one worker for the duration of the message — choose based on your expected concurrent message volume.

allowedValues.label: Starter (8 workers) — up to 8 concurrent transformations per instance
Professional (32 workers) — up to 32 concurrent transformations per instance
Enterprise (64 workers) — up to 64 concurrent transformations per instance
Enterprise+ (128 workers) — up to 128 concurrent transformations per instance

elements.name: cpuCores
elements.label: CPU Cores per instance
elements.toolTip: CPU allocation per container instance. Higher values support more concurrent transformations.

elements.name: memoryGi
elements.label: Memory per instance
elements.toolTip: Memory allocation per container instance. XML/JSON transformation expands messages 10-50× in memory. Choose based on your largest message size.

allowedValues.label: 8 GB — messages up to 1 MB+ (large payloads)

options.text: XML and JSON messages expand 10-50x in memory during transformation (UDM parsing). A 100 KB XML message uses ~5-10 MB of heap. With 8 concurrent workers, choose memory accordingly. For messages over 1 MB, use 8 GB.

elements.name: minReplicas
elements.label: Minimum instances
elements.toolTip: Set to 0 for scale-to-zero (no cost when idle, ~10s cold start). Set to 1 for always-on (recommended for production).

elements.name: maxReplicas
elements.label: Maximum instances
elements.toolTip: Maximum number of instances for auto-scaling under load.
